bindingForm.iobDataTypeHint: Automatic is usually enough. For lights/switches: Boolean.
bindingForm.iobJsonKeyHint: Optional when the state itself contains a JSON object.
bindingForm.iobCommandStateHint: Only needed when status and command use separate states.
bindingForm.ztMetaHint: Updated automatically at startup and daily at midnight.
bindingForm.ztHolidaysHint: Triggers only on selected holidays. No selection = every holiday.

bindingForm.minDeltaHint: Empty = inactive. If both are set, both must match. Numeric values only.

form.hostHint: IP address of the KNX/IP interface

docker.warningDescription: In a Docker bridge network, xknx selects the container IP instead of the host LAN IP. KNX IP Secure (TCP) still sends UDP requests during connection setup that cannot be answered without host networking. Solution: set network_mode: host in docker-compose.yml.

serialize_reads.title: Serialize reads
serialize_reads.description: Sends Modbus requests one at a time instead of concurrently. Recommended for simple devices (heating controllers, inverters, meters) that can only handle one request at a time. Disable for capable PLCs with multi-request support.

startup_jitter_s.title: Startup jitter (s)
startup_jitter_s.description: Maximum random delay (seconds) before the first poll of each binding. Prevents a request burst when all tasks start simultaneously. 0 = disabled.

filter.onlyOnChangeTrigger: Only trigger if value has changed (no duplicate)

ical.refreshHint: Calendar is reloaded at most every N minutes.
ical.rawInfo: The RAW output always delivers the raw iCal text. Filters can be added for extended analysis.
ical.noFilters: No filters yet. Each filter provides 4 outputs: Array, Next Date, Tomorrow, Today.
ical.reSyntax: Python re syntax. Empty = field is ignored.

nodeDescriptions.const_value: Outputs a fixed value — number, bool or text. Useful as threshold or reference.
nodeDescriptions.and: Output is true when ALL inputs are true. Inputs (2–30) and output can be individually negated.
nodeDescriptions.or: Output is true when AT LEAST ONE input is true. Inputs (2–30) and output can be individually negated.
nodeDescriptions.xor: Output is true when EXACTLY ONE input is true. Inputs (2–30) and output can be individually negated.
nodeDescriptions.gate: Signal gate: passes the input when enable=true, blocks otherwise. Behavior when closed: hold the last value (retain) or output a default value.
nodeDescriptions.hysteresis: Switches ON when threshold is exceeded, OFF only when it falls below
nodeDescriptions.datapoint_read: Outputs the current value of a DataPoint. Triggers on value change.
nodeDescriptions.datapoint_write: Writes a value to a DataPoint
nodeDescriptions.math_formula: Evaluates an expression. Variables: a (= IN 1), b (= IN 2)
nodeDescriptions.math_map: Scales a value from one range to another
nodeDescriptions.clamp: Limits the input value to [Min, Max]. Values outside the range are clamped to the boundary.
nodeDescriptions.random_value: Outputs a random value between Min and Max on each trigger signal. Type 'int' returns an integer (random.randint), type 'float' returns a floating-point number with configurable decimal places.
nodeDescriptions.statistics: Continuously calculates Min/Max/Average over all received values. Reset input resets the statistics.
nodeDescriptions.avg_multi: Calculates the average of 2–20 inputs (current) and moving averages over time windows: 1 min, 1 h, 1 day, 7/14/30/180/365 days. Each new value is stored with a timestamp. Time window values are retained after restart (configurable).
nodeDescriptions.string_concat: Concatenates 2–20 texts into one result. Each input can be dynamically connected or statically preset. A connected input takes precedence over the static text.
nodeDescriptions.heating_circuit: Summer/winter switching according to DIN (Mannheim method). Input: outdoor temperature. Measurement times (first crossing): T1 = value from 07:00, T2 = from 14:00, T3 = from 21:00. Works even if the sensor does not hit the measurement hours exactly. Each slot is recorded only once per day. Daily average: T_avg = (T1 + T2 + 2×T3) / 4. Monthly average: moving average of the last 31 daily averages. Heating mode ON when T_avg < threshold, OFF when T_avg ≥ threshold + hysteresis. Missing slots are filled from history on startup. State is retained across restarts.
nodeDescriptions.min_max_tracker: Tracks minimum and maximum over time periods (daily, weekly, monthly, yearly, absolute). Period values are automatically reset at day/week/month/year boundaries.
nodeDescriptions.consumption_counter: Calculates consumption values (daily, weekly, monthly, yearly) from a continuously incrementing counter value. Also stores the previous period's consumption for comparisons.
nodeDescriptions.timer_delay: Delays a signal by N seconds
nodeDescriptions.timer_pulse: Outputs a pulse for N seconds
nodeDescriptions.timer_cron: Fires automatically on a cron schedule (minute hour day month weekday).
nodeDescriptions.operating_hours: Counts operating hours while 'Active' is true. Reset clears the counter.
nodeDescriptions.python_script: Executes a Python script. Available: inputs dict → return value
nodeDescriptions.astro_sun: Calculates sunrise and sunset based on latitude/longitude. Requires: pip install astral
nodeDescriptions.notify_pushover: Sends a push notification via the Pushover API (api.pushover.net). Triggered automatically when a message arrives at the input.
nodeDescriptions.notify_sms: Sends an SMS via the seven.io gateway (gateway.seven.io). Triggered automatically when a message arrives at the input.
nodeDescriptions.json_extractor: Parses a JSON string and extracts one or more values by key paths (dot notation, e.g. sensors.temperature). Multiple outputs configurable via + in the config panel.
nodeDescriptions.xml_extractor: Parses an XML string and extracts one or more values by XPath expressions (ElementTree syntax, e.g. .//temperature). Multiple outputs configurable via + in the config panel.
nodeDescriptions.substring_extractor: Extracts text from a string using substring operations or regular expressions. Modes: left_of / right_of (first or last occurrence), between (two markers), cut (position + length), regex (Python re syntax, capture groups selectable).
nodeDescriptions.ical: Loads an iCal/ICS file from a URL and evaluates events. The RAW output delivers the raw calendar text. Each filter provides 4 outputs: Array (all events), Next date, Tomorrow (bool), Today (bool). Filters can be applied to Summary, Location and/or Description.
nodeDescriptions.api_client: Sends HTTP requests (GET/POST/PUT…) to external APIs. The trigger input controls execution.

general.title: General settings
general.timezoneHint: All timestamps in the system are shown in this timezone.
general.appearanceHint: Choose how the interface should be displayed.

security.title: URL target allowlist
security.description: Logic nodes and API proxies may call public HTTP/HTTPS targets directly. Internal, private, or reserved IP ranges are blocked unless they are explicitly listed in this YAML allowlist.
security.warning: Allowing a target lets the backend actively request that internal address. Use this only for trusted systems on your own network; broad or incorrect entries can weaken SSRF protection and network segmentation.

support.title: Support package
support.description: The support package is generated locally and downloaded. It is not sent automatically; sensitive values are centrally redacted before export.
support.exportFailed: Support package could not be created.
support.debugDescription: Debug logging collects more detailed local diagnostic entries for a short time so they are included in the support package created afterwards.
support.debugFlow1: Enable debug, reproduce the issue and then create the support package. The in-memory log buffer is included in the package.
support.debugFlow2: Nothing is sent and no remote access is opened. After expiry or manual deactivation, the previous log level is restored.
support.viewerDescription: Open an obs_support JSON file locally and inspect it in a structured view. The file is not uploaded or stored.
support.viewerInvalid: The file is not a valid support package.
support.viewerNoWarnings: No warnings or errors in package.
support.viewerLogFilterHint: Combine multiple search fragments with spaces. A trailing * matches prefixes.
support.viewerNoLogMatches: No log entries match the filter.

installation.description: Installation type, OBS version, operating system and runtime environment.

health.description: Runtime health states, uptime and last start timestamp.

apikeys.newKeySecret: ⚠ Key visible only now — copy it now!

importexport.exportDesc: Save all objects, bindings, adapter instances, KNX group addresses, logic sheets, Visu, NavLinks, AppSettings, Hierarchy, Icons and the FontAwesome API Key as a JSON file.
importexport.importDesc: Apply a JSON backup file. Existing entries are updated, missing ones created. Visu, NavLinks, Icons and all other configuration data are also restored.
importexport.dbExportDesc: Save the complete SQLite database (incl. history data and all configurations) as a file.
importexport.dbImportDesc: Upload and apply a SQLite database file.
importexport.dbImportWarning: ⚠ Warning — this action overwrites all current data!
importexport.dbImportWarning1: All current objects, bindings, adapters, Visu, logic sheets will be replaced
importexport.dbImportWarning2: History data will be replaced by the content of the uploaded file
importexport.dbImportWarning3: Adapters and Logic Engine will be restarted after restore
importexport.dbImportWarning4: Data not included in the backup will be lost
importexport.autobackupDesc: Daily automatic JSON backup of all configuration data (adapters, objects, logic module, Visu, icons, etc.). Backups are stored locally in the data directory.
importexport.autobackupRestoreDesc: Select a saved autobackup and apply it (upsert semantics — same as regular restore).
importexport.autobackupRestoreWarning1: Existing entries are updated, missing ones created
importexport.autobackupRestoreWarning2: Entries deleted since the backup will be preserved
importexport.autobackupRestoreWarning3: For a complete restore: first factory reset, then restore
importexport.autobackupRestoreOk: Restore OK: {objects} objects, {bindings} bindings, {visu} Visu nodes
importexport.knxDesc: Import an ETS project file. All group addresses (GA, name, DPT) are saved, available as suggestions in the binding form and included in backups.
importexport.knxNoAdapter: No KNX adapter instance found. Please create a KNX adapter first.
importexport.error_INVALID_PASSWORD: Wrong password or file is encrypted.
importexport.error_NO_GROUP_ADDRESSES: No group addresses found. Please export the correct ETS project.
importexport.dbImportResultOk: Database restore OK: {message} ({adapters} adapters restarted). Please reload the page.

history.dbDesc: Choose where historical values are stored. Changes take effect immediately (no restart required).
history.defaultWindowHoursHint: Applies to history API calls without a from parameter.
history.filterDesc: Objects with history disabled are not stored in the history DB. Typical candidates: time, date, system values without historical relevance.

icons.empty: No icons installed yet. Upload SVG files to get started.
icons.importDesc: Upload SVG files or a ZIP archive with SVG icons. Each file is checked for valid SVG format regardless of file extension.
icons.faDesc: Import icons directly from FontAwesome 7 Free. Without an API Key, free icons are used; with an API Key, PRO icons and additional styles are available. The filename automatically includes the style (e.g. abacus-solid.svg).
icons.faApiKeyHint: The key is stored persistently and automatically used on every import.
icons.knxufDesc: Downloads all icons from the ha-knx-uf-iconset directly from the GitHub repository and saves them with the prefix kuf_ in the icon library. Existing kuf_ icons will be overwritten.

links.title: Custom Links
links.desc: Appear in the sidebar navigation (visible to all users).
links.label: Label
links.requiredFields: Label and URL are required fields.

bindings.label: Delete all bindings
bindings.desc: Deletes all bindings. Objects and adapter instances are preserved.
bindings.confirmMessage: All bindings will be permanently deleted. Objects and adapter instances are preserved. Continue?

datapoints.label: Delete all objects
datapoints.desc: Permanently deletes all objects and their bindings.
datapoints.confirmMessage: All objects and their bindings will be permanently deleted. Continue?

logic.label: Delete all logic sheets
logic.desc: Deletes all logic sheets and stops the Logic Engine.
logic.confirmMessage: All logic sheets will be permanently deleted. Continue?

adapters.label: Delete all adapters
adapters.desc: Stops and deletes all adapter instances and their bindings.
adapters.confirmMessage: All adapter instances and their bindings will be permanently deleted. Continue?

knxga.label: Delete KNX group addresses
knxga.desc: Deletes all imported KNX group addresses ({n} GAs).
knxga.confirmMessage: All imported KNX group addresses will be deleted. Continue?

factory.label: Reset to Factory Settings
factory.desc: Deletes everything — objects, bindings, adapters, KNX GAs, logic sheets, icons and the FontAwesome API Key. User accounts are preserved.
factory.confirmMessage: All objects, bindings, adapter instances, KNX group addresses, logic sheets, icons and the FontAwesome API Key will be permanently deleted. User accounts are preserved. Continue?
factory.result: Reset: {dp} objects, {bindings} bindings, {adapters} adapters, {knxga} KNX GAs, {logic} logic sheets deleted.

common.demoMode: Demo mode — this area is read-only/locked.
common.adapterOrphanTitle: Adapter is no longer configured — filter will not match new entries.

hierarchy.title: Device structure
hierarchy.displayStartLevelHelp: Determines which level is shown in the abbreviated path tag. The full path is always visible as a tooltip.
hierarchy.importDescription: Building/trade mode uses the spatial or functional structure from the ETS project.
hierarchy.perspectiveHelp: Choose the perspective according to the structure of your ETS project, or enter a custom name below.
hierarchy.modeTopology2LevelMid: 2-level: Main group → Middle group (without GA leaves)
hierarchy.confirmDeleteHierarchy: This hierarchy and all its nodes and links will be permanently deleted.
hierarchy.confirmDeleteNode: This node and all sub-nodes and links will be permanently deleted.
